assertion_results.text: Data extraction correctly identifies CVE-2026-31812, library quinn-proto, affected range < 0.11.14, and fixed version 0.11.14 from the Vulnerability issue (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md table shows: CVE ID = CVE-2026-31812, Vulnerable library = quinn-proto, Affected version range = 'versions before 0.11.14 (< 0.11.14)', Fixed version = 0.11.14. All four fields are correctly extracted.

assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as Cargo based on the library name and component context (Step 1 Ecosystem detection)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md Ecosystem Detection section states: 'Ecosystem: Cargo (Rust crate) — quinn-proto is a Rust crate, identified from the library name and the Cargo ecosystem mapping in the stream's security-matrix.md'. Lock file identified as Cargo.lock.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the security-matrix.md supportability matrix — no versions are skipped (Important Rule 4)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md Section 2.4 Version Impact Table lists all 7 versions: 2.1.0, 2.1.1 (from stream 2.1.x) and 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4 (from stream 2.2.x). Both streams are fully represented, matching the supportability matrix shown in Section 2.1.

assertion_results.text: Version impact analysis uses pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix for each version, not branch HEAD (Important Rule 13)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md Section 2.3 shows extraction using specific tags per version: v0.3.8 for 2.1.0, v0.3.12 for 2.1.1, v0.4.5 for 2.2.0, v0.4.8 for 2.2.1, v0.4.9 for 2.2.2 (noted as retag), v0.4.11 for 2.2.3, v0.4.12 for 2.2.4. The check command template is 'git show <tag>:Cargo.lock | grep -A2 "name = \"quinn-proto\""'. These tags correspond to the pinned backend column values in the supportability matrix.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table identifies retag versions and carries forward the affected status from the original version (Important Rule 5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md shows version 2.2.2 with tag v0.4.9, quinn-proto listed as '—', Affected = YES, and Notes = 'retag of 2.2.1'. The supportability matrix in Section 2.1 confirms 2.2.2 uses backend tag v0.4.8 (same as 2.2.1), and Section 2.3 notes '(same as 2.2.1: quinn-proto 0.11.12)'. The affected status is correctly carried forward from 2.2.1.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction proposes replacing PSIRT-assigned versions with lock-file-verified versions, showing the diff (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md Section 3.2 Correction shows: Current = '[RHTPA 2.0.0]', Problem = 'RHTPA 2.0.0 does not correspond to any configured Version Stream', Proposed = '[RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2]'. The proposed comment also shows the diff: 'Corrected Affects Versions: [RHTPA 2.0.0] -> [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2]'.

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction references version names from the supportability matrix rather than hardcoded Jira version IDs (Important Rule 6)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md Section 3.1 lists Jira IDs as '(dynamic)' for all versions and explicitly states 'Jira version IDs would be discovered dynamically at runtime — never hardcoded.' Section 3.2 Proposed Jira Mutation uses placeholder IDs like '<RHTPA-2.2.0-jira-id>' rather than hardcoded numeric IDs. The correction references version names (RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2) throughout.

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include Repository, Target Branch, acceptance criteria, and dependency linkage between upstream and downstream tasks (Important Rule 9)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 1 description includes: Repository = 'rhtpa-backend', Target Branch = 'release/0.4.z', Acceptance Criteria with checkboxes. Task 2 includes: Repository = 'rhtpa-release.0.4.z', Target Branch = 'main', Acceptance Criteria with checkboxes. Dependency linkage is shown in 'Jira Linkage for 2.2.x Tasks' section with jira.create_link calls including Blocks link between upstream and downstream tasks.

assertion_results.text: For Cargo ecosystem, remediation creates two tasks: upstream backport task and downstream propagation subtask with Blocks dependency (Important Rule 8)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md creates Task 1 (upstream backport: 'Remediate CVE-2026-31812: bump quinn-proto to 0.11.14') and Task 2 (downstream propagation: 'Propagate CVE-2026-31812 fix: update rhtpa-backend ref in rhtpa-release.0.4.z'). The Jira Linkage section includes: 'jira.create_link(inwardIssue: <upstream-task-key>, outwardIssue: <downstream-task-key>, type: "Blocks")' establishing the Blocks dependency.

assertion_results.text: The analysis presents triage recommendations as proposed actions — framing Affects Versions changes, label additions, and status transitions as proposals rather than executed mutations (Guardrails)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md frames the correction as 'Proposed' (Section 3.2 header 'Proposed Jira Mutation' with 'After engineer confirmation:'). remediation.md presents Jira mutations as code blocks showing what would be called, not as executed actions — e.g., 'jira.create_issue(...)' and 'jira.transition_issue(...)' are shown as proposed calls. The affects-versions.md Correction section uses language 'Proposed: [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2]'. The status transition to 'In Progress' and label additions are presented as planned mutations in the Post-Triage Summary section, not as already-executed actions.
